findings.message: frontmatter has `license: Unknown` — the benchling-sdk Python package is MIT licensed per PyPI
SKILL.md states 'Nano Banana Pro will automatically generate, review, and refine the schematic' as a standard workflow step, requiring undisclosed paid API key
Installation commands in Dependencies section use bare `pip install requests`, `pip install bibtexparser`, etc. instead of `uv pip install`
SKILL.md mandates 'Nano Banana Pro will automatically generate' schematics, requiring undisclosed paid API credentials
frontmatter has `license: Unknown` — the DNAnexus Python SDK is Apache-2.0 licensed. This field should be resolvable with a quick check of the library's repository.
frontmatter has no `metadata:` block, so `skill-author` is absent. Provenance and attribution cannot be determined.
Installation command reads `uv uv pip install fluidsim` (appears 3 times in installation section) — the doubled `uv` prefix is an invalid shell invocation
SKILL.md is the wrapper for 'Nano Banana 2' image generation. Skill description does not disclose the required OPENROUTER_API_KEY paid credential.
Installation commands read `uv uv pip install geniml`, `uv uv pip install 'geniml[ml]'`, and `uv uv pip install git+https://...` — doubled `uv` prefix is invalid in all three forms
frontmatter has `license: Unknown` — glycoSHIELD is MIT licensed per its GitHub repository
Installation command reads `uv uv pip install gtars` — the doubled `uv` prefix is an invalid shell invocation
frontmatter has `license: Unknown` — gtars is MIT licensed per its GitHub repository
frontmatter has `license: Unknown` — LabArchive is a commercial platform; the REST API client license should be confirmed with vendor
Installation command reads `python3 -m uv pip install latch` — calling uv as a Python module is not the standard invocation and may fail depending on how uv is installed
SKILL.md references 'Nano Banana Pro' for poster graphic generation as a standard tool without disclosing API key requirement. Also missing license field and metadata.skill-author.
frontmatter has no `metadata:` block (no skill-author) and also no `license` field at all
SKILL.md mandates Nano Banana Pro for figure generation without disclosing required paid API key
SKILL.md contains a brief Nano Banana Pro reference for optional diagram generation. Also uses `pip install` not `uv pip install`.
Installation commands use bare `pip install` instead of `uv pip install`
frontmatter has `license: Unknown` — OMERO.py is GPL-2.0 licensed
frontmatter has `license: Unknown` — the Opentrons Python SDK is Apache-2.0 licensed
frontmatter has no `license` field
frontmatter has no `metadata:` block, so `skill-author` is absent. File also uses `license: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms` which is non-standard.
SKILL.md mandates 'Nano Banana Pro will automatically generate' schematics without disclosing required paid API key
frontmatter has `license: Unknown` — the referenced tools (MUSCLE, IQ-TREE, BioPython) have various OSS licenses
frontmatter `license` field contains a URL rather than an SPDX identifier
frontmatter `license` field contains a URL rather than an SPDX identifier
SKILL.md mandates Nano Banana Pro for diagram generation without disclosing required paid API key
frontmatter has `license: Unknown` — the protocols.io Python client is MIT licensed
Installation command reads `uv uv pip install pyopenms` — the doubled `uv` prefix is an invalid shell invocation that will fail immediately for users copying this command

Script sends user research query text to https://api.parallel.ai (PARALLEL_API_KEY) and optionally to OpenRouter Perplexity backend (OPENROUTER_API_KEY). Two separate commercial services receive user query content. The research-lookup SKILL.md description does not disclose that queries are transmitted externally.
frontmatter contains `license: SD-3-Clause license` — the leading `B` is missing from `BSD-3-Clause`. This is an apparent typo that results in an unrecognized SPDX identifier.
SKILL.md mandates Nano Banana Pro for scientific schematic generation without disclosing the required API key
SKILL.md references 'Nano Banana Pro' for diagram generation and 'Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview' for quality review — both proprietary paid services not disclosed in description

SKILL.md contains mandatory workflow instructions referencing 'Nano Banana 2' and 'Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview' — K-Dense Inc.'s proprietary paid AI image generation product. Skills invoking this workflow fail silently without an undisclosed paid API key (OPENROUTER_API_KEY). The description does not mention this requirement.

Script makes POST requests to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 transmitting user diagram prompt text and receiving AI-generated images. API key sourced from OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var (correct practice). Risk: user natural-language prompts leave the local machine to a commercial third-party service. Skill descriptions referencing this script do not disclose data transmission.
SKILL.md mandates Nano Banana Pro for figure generation without disclosing required paid API key
Installation command reads `uv uv pip install scikit-learn` — the doubled `uv` prefix is an invalid shell invocation that will fail immediately for users copying this command
Installation command reads `pip install scvelo` instead of the collection standard `uv pip install scvelo`
frontmatter `license` field contains a URL rather than an SPDX identifier
frontmatter has no `license` field — PyTorch Geometric is MIT licensed

SKILL.md contains mandatory instructions to call 'Nano Banana Pro' for schematic generation, implying this is a standard capability when it requires a paid API key not disclosed in the skill description
